utility_evaluation.REASONING:
There are 5 items requested in the final format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with all required details:
- health_coverage: Requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. Plan provides physical_health with plan name and €100/month, mental_health with plan name and €65/month, and a combined cost €165/month. Fully specified.
- property_coverage: Requires home/contents insurance with costs. Plan provides home_content plan name (EliteHome Premium Coverage), description, and €95/month. Fully specified.
- travel_coverage: Requires travel insurance with costs. Plan provides plan name, details, and €50/month. Fully specified.
- additional_services: Requires any enhancement services with costs. Plan includes a specialized emergency contact service but no cost is provided. Not fully specified because cost is missing.
- total_budget: Requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. Plan gives €310/month but no annual breakdown. The format explicitly says complete monthly/annual cost breakdown; annual is missing, so not fully specified.
Thus, 3 items are fully specified (health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage) out of 5.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes three insurances: LifeFirst Standard Plan for physical health, LifeFirst Standard Plan for mental health, EliteHome Premium Coverage for home contents, and LifeFirst Standard Plan for travel insurance. I matched each to the corresponding entries in the ground-truth ratings under primary_health_coverage (physical_health and mental_health), property_protection (home_content), and mobility_coverage (travel_insurance).
